name: West Bengal
stopoverNote: Kolkata (CCU) is the main gateway; Bagdogra (IXB) is the practical exit for Darjeeling/Kalimpong. Kolkata→Darjeeling is a long multi-leg journey — use the overnight Sealdah→NJP train plus a hill taxi, or fly to Bagdogra and drive up.

diet.vegetarian: Bengali cuisine is fish-heavy, but vegetarian travellers can eat well in Kolkata and the hills. Look for shukto, aloo posto, cholar dal, luchi, dhokar dalna, mochar ghonto, chhachchari, veg momos, thukpa and Nepali dal-bhat. Sundarbans is remote, so pre-book veg meals with the boat/lodge operator and carry snacks.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is workable in Kolkata but harder outside it. Ask for mustard oil instead of ghee and avoid paneer, curd and milk-based gravies. Bengali sweets such as rosogolla, sandesh and mishti doi are dairy-based and usually not vegan; Darjeeling/Kalimpong have limited vegan options, so self-catering snacks help.
phrases: Maachh/mangsho/chingri chara = without fish, meat or prawn
Ghee, doodh, doi, paneer chara = without ghee, milk, curd or paneer
Shorsher tel-e banaben = please cook in mustard oil

cities.name: Kolkata
cities.notes: India's cultural capital — British colonial grandeur, Tagore's Bengal Renaissance, riverfront heritage and South Asia's most passionate food culture.

cities.name: Sundarbans
cities.notes: The world's largest mangrove delta — a UNESCO World Heritage Site and Ramsar wetland for boat safaris, crocodiles, dolphins, birds and rare Royal Bengal Tiger sightings.

cities.name: Darjeeling
cities.notes: The Queen of the Hills at 2,042 m — UNESCO toy train, Kanchenjunga sunrise viewpoints, Tibetan-Bengali hill culture and world-famous tea.

cities.name: Kalimpong
cities.notes: A quieter Himalayan town with monasteries, colonial-era Morgan House, orchid nurseries and Teesta valley views without Darjeeling's crowds.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well in Kolkata and the plains; buy at the airport or an authorised store with ID. Coverage weakens inside Sundarbans channels and on hill roads around Darjeeling/Kalimpong.

Kolkata.name: Kolkata
Kolkata.note: Three days gives Kolkata its due — one colonial waterfront day, one North Kolkata food/temple day, and one culture buffer before the Sundarbans leg.

days.note: Victoria Memorial, Maidan, the Indian Museum and the riverfront sit in a manageable central arc. Start at 10am because Victoria Memorial and Indian Museum are closed Mondays.

activities.name: Victoria Memorial museum and gardens
activities.tip: Closed Mondays. The evening light-and-sound show is about ₹100 when running; arrive near opening for quieter galleries.

activities.name: Indian Museum — Egyptian mummy, Indus and natural history galleries
activities.tip: Closed Mondays; focus on the historic core galleries rather than trying to see everything.

activities.name: Howrah Bridge walk and Mullick Ghat flower market
activities.tip: Best at dawn, but still atmospheric later; use the pedestrian path and keep valuables tucked away.

activities.name: Prinsep Ghat sunset by the Hooghly

days.theme: North Kolkata lanes, temples & Bengali food
days.note: North Kolkata's lanes are better on foot than by cab. Combine Dakshineswar and Belur Math early, then return through Kumartuli and College Street.

activities.name: Dakshineswar Kali Temple morning visit
activities.tip: Go 6–8am for aarti and river calm; the temple closes for a midday break.

activities.name: Belur Math by ferry or short road hop
activities.tip: The architecture intentionally blends Hindu, Islamic and Gothic forms.

activities.name: Kumartuli potters' quarter
activities.tip: Most atmospheric before Durga Puja, but artisans work year-round.

activities.name: College Street and Indian Coffee House
Park Street food evening
activities.tip: Kathi rolls cost roughly ₹80–₹140; vegetarians can do paneer/eggless versions.

hotels.name: Backpacker hostels near Park Street/Sudder Street

meals: Peter Cat or Mocambo for old Kolkata atmosphere

days.note: Use this as a softer cultural day and Sundarbans prep day. If Marble Palace matters to you, arrange the free WBTDC NOC one or two days ahead.

activities.name: Jorasanko Thakur Bari / Rabindra Bharati Museum
activities.tip: Closed Mondays; best for Tagore and Bengal Renaissance context.

activities.name: Marble Palace
activities.tip: Permit process is the real constraint, not the entry fee.

activities.name: Victoria Memorial light-and-sound show
activities.tip: Useful if you skipped the evening show on Day 1; not available Mondays.

meals: The Flaming Bowl for vegan food
Balaram Mullick for dairy sweets (not vegan)

Sundarbans.name: Sundarbans
Sundarbans.note: Treat the Sundarbans as a permit-and-boat trip, not a casual road excursion. One night at a lodge or boat package is much more realistic than a rushed day trip.

days.note: Independent core-zone entry is not practical: book a registered operator or arrange Forest Department permits, boat and guide in advance. Tiger sightings are rare; deer, crocodiles and birds are the honest baseline.

activities.name: Boat transit to Sajnekhali Sanctuary and Watch Tower
activities.tip: Sajnekhali also has a crocodile enclosure and small museum.

activities.name: Sudhanyakhali Watch Tower boat safari
activities.tip: Sit quietly by the freshwater pool; this is one of the better tiger-sighting points, but sightings are never guaranteed.

activities.name: Bali Island village walk
activities.tip: Ask respectfully about honey collectors and tiger-widow communities; avoid intrusive photography.

hotels.name: Sundarbans Jungle Camp / similar lodge package

meals: Carry fruit, biscuits and water from Kolkata

days.theme: Dobanki canopy walk & return to Kolkata
days.note: Depart the forest by mid-afternoon if you need the overnight NJP train. Monsoon months are unsafe and core zones may be closed, especially July–August.

activities.name: Dobanki Canopy Walk
activities.tip: The 1.1 km raised walkway is excellent for birds and mangrove perspective.

activities.name: Netidhopani Watch Tower
activities.tip: Choose only if your operator confirms tide, permit and timing feasibility.

activities.name: Return boat to Godkhali and cab/train to Kolkata
activities.tip: Aim for Sealdah by evening for Darjeeling Mail / Kanchan Kanya connections.

hotels.name: Overnight train to NJP after returning to Kolkata

meals: Dinner near Sealdah before the train

Darjeeling.name: Darjeeling
Darjeeling.note: Three days covers the sunrise, UNESCO toy train, tea estate, monasteries and a flexible hill-walk or transfer buffer. Monsoon landslides can disrupt roads and toy-train service.

days.theme: Kanchenjunga sunrise, town & UNESCO toy train
days.note: This is an early-start day. Tiger Hill requires a 3:45–4:00am hotel pickup; book the toy train joy ride on IRCTC before you arrive.

activities.name: Tiger Hill sunrise from Observatory Tower
activities.tip: Dress for 0–5°C and buy the higher-floor tower ticket for less obstructed views.

activities.name: Darjeeling Himalayan Railway Joy Ride to Ghoom
activities.tip: Steam runs on selected services; IRCTC is the definitive fare/schedule source.

activities.name: Batasia Loop and War Memorial Garden
activities.tip: Included as a stop on many joy rides, but also visitable independently.

activities.name: Chowrasta Mall Road and Observatory Hill

days.note: Happy Valley is close to town; combine the zoo/HMI cluster with Ghoom Monastery by taxi for an efficient hill day.

activities.name: Happy Valley Tea Estate guided factory tour
activities.tip: Open Tue–Sun, 8am–4pm; factory may close Nov–Feb off-season.

activities.name: Ghoom Monastery (Yiga Choeling)
activities.tip: Oldest gompa in the district, with a 5-metre Maitreya Buddha.

activities.name: Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park + HMI Museum
activities.cost: Zoo ₹60 adult / ₹25 child (Indian); ₹100 foreign; HMI included
activities.tip: See red panda and snow leopard conservation exhibits plus Tenzing Norgay mountaineering history.

activities.name: Darjeeling Ropeway
activities.tip: Operational status changes; verify locally before relying on it.

days.theme: Hill nature walk or Kalimpong transfer
days.note: Use the morning for a short nature outing if weather is clear, then leave by 10am if continuing to Kalimpong. Roads are narrow and landslide-prone in monsoon.

activities.name: Rock Garden and Ganga Maya Park
activities.tip: Go early before crowds; the waterfall terraces are better after some rain but not during active monsoon disruptions.

activities.name: Tensing Rock beginner climbing session
Depart for Kalimpong via Teesta Bazaar
activities.tip: Leave by late morning to enjoy Teesta valley views and still sightsee in Kalimpong.

meals: Veg thukpa or momos before departure

Kalimpong.name: Kalimpong
Kalimpong.note: Kalimpong works as a quieter one- or two-night hill stop after Darjeeling, with monasteries, orchid nurseries, colonial bungalows and an easy Bagdogra departure.

days.note: The main sights form a compact 5 km hill-town triangle. Hire a local taxi for a half-day rather than walking the steep road links.

activities.name: Durpin Monastery and Durpin Dara viewpoint
activities.tip: Consecrated by the Dalai Lama; morning gives the best Teesta valley and Kanchenjunga views.

activities.name: Morgan House heritage bungalow
activities.cost: Garden/visit usually free; stay rates vary
activities.tip: A WBTDCL heritage property — check current access if not staying there.

activities.name: Cactus and orchid nurseries on Nursery Road
activities.tip: Kalimpong is a major orchid and cactus supplier; ask before photographing inside private nurseries.

activities.name: Deolo Hill viewpoint
activities.tip: Sunset is good; paragliding varies roughly ₹1,500–₹2,500 when operating.

activities.name: Tharpa Choling Monastery

days.theme: Old school campus, bazaar & Bagdogra departure
days.note: Kalimpong to Bagdogra/NJP is about two hours in normal weather; leave by 7am for 10am-or-later flights. Monsoon Teesta flooding can add delays.

activities.name: Dr. Graham's Homes campus
activities.tip: A 1900 Scottish missionary school with broad Himalayan views.

activities.name: Kalimpong Haat Bazaar
activities.tip: Best on Thursday and Sunday for cardamom, dried ginger, shawls and Tibetan crafts.

activities.name: Transfer to Bagdogra Airport or NJP
activities.tip: Bagdogra→Kolkata flights take about 1h10m and often cost ₹2,950–₹5,700.

meals: Tea and momos near the market

connections.method: Private car/cab to Godkhali ferry ghat, then boat into Sajnekhali or lodge area
connections.cost: Cab ₹2,500–₹3,500 one-way; train to Canning ₹25 + van to Godkhali ₹150/person

connections.method: Return by boat/road to Kolkata, overnight Sealdah→NJP train, then shared/private taxi to Darjeeling; faster alternative is CCU→Bagdogra flight plus hill taxi
connections.cost: Train ₹360–₹2,335; NJP shared taxi ~₹1,000/person; flight alternative ₹2,950–₹5,700 + cab ₹2,000–₹2,500

connections.method: Shared cab or private taxi via Teesta Bazaar

extras: Victoria Memorial prices verified from the research: museum ₹50 for Indian nationals / ₹500 for foreign nationals, garden ₹30; closed Mondays.
Darjeeling Himalayan Railway joy ride prices verified from the research: diesel ₹800–₹1,000, steam ₹1,300–₹1,600, vistadome ₹1,200–₹1,600; book on IRCTC and re-check actual fare on travel date.

Sundarbans is permit- and boat-dependent: expect forest entry ₹120–₹180/person/day, boat permit ₹1,000–₹1,200/day, guide ₹600–₹850/day and non-AC boat hire ₹6,000–₹10,000/day for a group. Packages from Kolkata typically run ₹4,500–₹8,000/person/day.

Kolkata→Darjeeling is not a same-day sightseeing transfer. Darjeeling Mail or Kanchan Kanya overnight train to NJP plus a 3–4 hour hill drive is the practical budget route; book trains 60+ days ahead in peak seasons.

Avoid June–September for this combined route: the Sundarbans core zones may close and Darjeeling/Kalimpong roads face landslide risk. October–November is the best single window for both delta and Himalayan visibility.
Bengali food culture is fish-forward; vegetarians have plenty of choices, but vegans must explicitly avoid ghee, curd, paneer and dairy sweets such as rosogolla, sandesh and mishti doi.
